note_data.title: Economic Development
keyPoints: **Economic development** involves sustained increases in real GDP per capita and improvements in living standards including health, education and life expectancy
**Developed countries** have high GDP per capita, advanced technology, good infrastructure and high human development index scores
**Developing countries** have low GDP per capita, limited industrialization, poor infrastructure and rely heavily on primary sector production
**Poverty** occurs when people cannot afford basic necessities like food, shelter, clean water and healthcare
**Living standards** are measured by real income per capita, access to education, healthcare, clean water and life expectancy
**Human Development Index (HDI)** combines GDP per capita, life expectancy and education levels to measure development
**Primary sector dependence** makes developing countries vulnerable to price fluctuations and limits economic growth
**Infrastructure development** including roads, electricity and telecommunications is essential for economic growth
**Education and healthcare** investments increase productivity and improve quality of life in developing countries
**Income inequality** exists both within countries and between developed and developing nations

definitions.definition: A sustained increase in real GDP per capita accompanied by improvements in living standards including health, education and life expectancy
A situation where people cannot afford basic necessities such as food, shelter, clean water and healthcare
The level of material comfort and services available to individuals, measured by income, access to healthcare, education and other basic needs
A composite measure of development that combines GDP per capita, life expectancy at birth and education levels
A country with low GDP per capita, limited industrialization and heavy dependence on the primary sector

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Break down the causes and effects. Use 'This leads to...' or 'As a result...'
Give advantages AND disadvantages, then conclude which is more important

modelAnswer.question: Explain two problems faced by developing countries. [6 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: One problem is heavy dependence on the primary sector. This means developing countries rely mainly on agriculture and raw material extraction for income. This is a problem because primary products have volatile prices and low value-added, limiting economic growth and making the country vulnerable to external shocks. Another problem is lack of infrastructure. Many developing countries have poor roads, unreliable electricity and limited telecommunications. This restricts business development because firms cannot transport goods efficiently or operate productively, which limits investment and economic growth.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Identification of primary sector dependence
Mark 2: Explanation of why this is a problem
Mark 5: Explanation of why this is a problem

commonMistakes: Never confuse economic growth with economic development - growth is just GDP increase, development includes living standards
Students often think higher GDP automatically means better living standards - consider income distribution
Don't just list features of developing countries - always explain WHY they are problems
Avoid saying 'poor countries' - use 'developing countries' or 'low-income countries'
Never ignore non-monetary measures of development like HDI, literacy rates and life expectancy
examinerTips: This topic appears frequently in Paper 2 Section B - often worth 8+ marks
Always use specific examples from Pakistan or other countries you know
Link poverty to specific causes like unemployment, low wages or lack of education
Remember HDI has THREE components - income, health AND education
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: No specific diagram required, but may include bar charts comparing GDP per capita, HDI scores or development indicators between developed and developing countries
